title: Heterocentron subtriplinervium
taxonomy: Heterocentron subtriplinervium is classified within the family Melastomataceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. The genus Heterocentron is native to Central and South America, with Heterocentron subtriplinervium being one of its species. This plant is a shrub or small tree, typically growing up to a few meters in height. The specific classification of Heterocentron subtriplinervium includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, and class as Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes the order Myrtales, family Melastomataceae, and genus Heterocentron.
uses: Heterocentron subtriplinervium, a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in some regions for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Research has also suggested that Heterocentron subtriplinervium may have antioxidant and cytotoxic effects, which could be beneficial for treating various health conditions. intro: Heterocentron subtriplinervium, also known as the 'Fogela', is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to the treatment of various conditions, including infections and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Heterocentron subtriplinervium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, making it a common herbal remedy in various forms.
